title: Day 14: Conflict Enters Third Week — No Ceasefire In Sight; Lebanon 634 Dead, 800k Displaced
detail: The Iran-US/Israel war enters its third week with no signs of de-escalation. Key metrics as of Day 14: Iran: 2,800+ killed, 17,000+ injured, 3.2M displaced; Lebanon: 634+ killed (incl. 91 children), 1,586+ injured, 800,000+ displaced; Israel: 13 killed; US: 8 KIA, 140 wounded. CENTCOM has struck 6,000+ targets across Iran. Iran's ballistic missile rate down 92% from peak. 90+ Iranian naval vessels destroyed or damaged. Mojtaba Khamenei's first statement sets defiant, no-ceasefire tone. IEA's record 400M barrel reserve release has not prevented oil from spiking above $100/bbl. Six vessels struck in Persian Gulf in 48 hours. Trump says war will end 'soon' but demands unconditional surrender. Iran rejects any surrender terms.

title: Iran Warns of '$200 Oil'; IEA Calls Disruption 'Largest in History of Global Oil Market'
detail: Iran's military spokesperson Ebrahim Zolfaqari warned: 'Get ready for oil to be $200 a barrel,' as Mojtaba Khamenei's statement doubled down on keeping the Strait of Hormuz closed. The IEA called this the 'largest supply disruption in the history of the global oil market,' with roughly 20M bbl/day (20% of global oil consumption) normally transiting through Hormuz now blocked. Despite the record 400M barrel reserve release, Brent crude remains above $100/bbl. Only five oil tankers departed the Persian Gulf area in 24 hours while approximately 500 remain stranded. The US Energy Secretary confirmed the US Navy is 'not ready' to escort tankers through Hormuz. Saudi Aramco CEO previously warned of 'catastrophic consequences' for global energy markets. European gas prices remain sharply elevated after Iranian strikes on Qatari LNG facilities.

title: UNHCR: 3.2 Million Iranians Displaced — Largest Displacement Crisis in War
detail: The UN Refugee Agency (UNHCR) reported that as many as 3.2 million Iranians have been forced out of their homes since the US-Israel war began on February 28 — making it one of the largest displacement crises triggered by any conflict in recent memory. The figure compounds Iran's civilian toll: 1,348+ civilians killed (Iranian Red Crescent), 17,000+ injured, and an internet blackout now exceeding 290 consecutive hours that prevents full independent verification. The WHO has separately warned that polluted 'black rain' from strikes on fuel depots poses serious public health risks as toxic smoke from oil facility fires mixed with rainfall.
